cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with an easy walk along the creek trail. It's shaded, flat, and a genuinely nice way to ease into the day before it warms up.
cards.why: 59°F and sunny is basically perfect trail weather.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours wandering the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum. Real mummies, actual ancient artifacts, and a planetarium tucked inside a temple-style building in San Jose. It's genuinely one of the weirdest and coolest things in the South Bay.
cards.why: This is the kind of place you keep meaning to check out and never do — today's the day.

cards.name: Prophetic Anger: Exploring the Book of Susanna
cards.blurb: Catch the Prophetic Anger exhibit exploring the Book of Susanna at SCU. It's a thought-provoking look at a rarely examined biblical text, and it's completely free.
cards.why: It's an easy, interesting stop to break up the afternoon.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head to the Triton Museum of Art for the contemporary galleries and the sculpture garden out back. It's free, calm, and the outdoor space is worth the visit alone on a warm afternoon.
cards.why: Free art, no crowds, and a sculpture garden in good weather is a pretty solid combo.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the day at Santana Row. Stroll the boulevard while it's still warm, grab a table at Straits or Sino for dinner, and take your time with it.
cards.why: The Saturday evening scene here is hard to beat when the weather cooperates.

cards.name: Petals Creamery
cards.address: 5 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Petals Creamery in Los Gatos for a scoop or two. They're open late and the ice cream is worth the stop.
cards.why: A solid dessert to close out the night.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a walk along the creek trail. Easy, flat, and shaded, so the kids can run ahead while you wake up properly.
cards.why: 59 degrees in the morning is perfect walking weather before it warms up.

cards.name: Saratoga Farmers Market
cards.address: 14000 Fruitvale Ave, Saratoga
cards.blurb: Swing through the Saratoga Farmers Market and let the kids pick out something fun to snack on while you browse the stands.
cards.why: Fresh air, fresh food, and zero cost makes this a no-brainer morning stop.

cards.name: Youth Commission Multicultural Celebration
cards.address: Los Gatos Civic Center, Los Gatos, CA
cards.blurb: Head over to Los Gatos Civic Center for the Youth Commission Multicultural Celebration. Expect performances, cultural displays, and stuff the kids will actually remember.
cards.why: Free, community-run, and genuinely fun for families.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Vasona Lake is the move for the afternoon. Walk the lake path, let the kids burn some energy, and if they spot the little train they'll want a ride.
cards.why: It's a warm afternoon and this park was basically made for days like this.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: The Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum is wild in the best way. Real mummies, real artifacts, and hieroglyphics kids can stare at for way longer than you'd expect.
cards.why: This place genuinely surprises everyone who goes, especially kids who think museums are boring.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the day at Main Street Cupertino. Grab dinner at Lazy Dog, let the kids walk the outdoor plaza, and enjoy the evening while the temperature is still nice.
cards.why: Good food, open-air layout, and easy for families to unwind without rushing.

cards.name: Saratoga Farmers Market
cards.address: 14000 Fruitvale Ave, Saratoga
cards.blurb: Wander through the stalls at Saratoga Farmers Market, grab some fresh fruit, sample whatever looks good, and pick up something to take home.
cards.why: Perfect morning weather for an outdoor market before the crowds show up.

cards.name: Huge Paperback Book Sale
cards.blurb: Dig through tables of cheap paperbacks and see what jumps out. You'll almost definitely walk away with something you didn't expect to want.
cards.why: A free book sale is the kind of thing you stumble into and end up loving.

cards.name: Historic Saratoga Village
cards.address: Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll through Historic Saratoga Village, pop into the boutiques and galleries, and soak up the quiet small-town charm before lunch.
cards.why: It's a genuinely pleasant stretch to walk on a Saturday morning like this.

cards.name: The Bywater
cards.address: 532 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Head to The Bywater for lunch and try their Louisiana-inspired menu. Sit on the patio if you can grab a spot.
cards.why: It's a solid mid-range lunch with real personality, not just another salad-and-sandwich situation.

cards.name: Sanity Thai Massage
cards.address: 20 S Santa Cruz Ave STE 105, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Get a Thai massage at Sanity in Los Gatos. An hour here in the middle of the afternoon is a genuinely good idea.
cards.why: You've been on your feet all morning. This is the move.

cards.name: Girls Who Code
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Check out the Girls Who Code event at 110 E Main St in Los Gatos. Free, local, and worth a look.
cards.why: It's happening today and it costs nothing to swing by.

cards.name: First Born
cards.address: 160 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at First Born for drinks and dinner. It's a cool spot with a laid-back vibe that earns its evening crowd.
cards.why: A proper Saturday night closer in Los Gatos.

cards.name: Saratoga Farmers Market
cards.address: 14000 Fruitvale Ave, Saratoga
cards.blurb: Wander the stalls, let the kids pick out something fresh to snack on, and soak up that Saturday morning market vibe at Fruitvale Ave.
cards.why: Perfect weather to be outside and it's free, which is always a win.

cards.name: Huge Paperback Book Sale
cards.blurb: Dig through tables of cheap paperbacks and let the kids fill a bag with whatever catches their eye. Great finds for all ages.
cards.why: A book sale is a fun, low-key way to keep the morning rolling without spending much.

cards.name: Youth Commission Multicultural Celebration
cards.address: Los Gatos Civic Center, Los Gatos, CA
cards.blurb: Head over to the Los Gatos Civic Center for the Youth Commission Multicultural Celebration. Expect performances, food, and activities the kids will genuinely enjoy.
cards.why: It's a free community event designed with kids in mind.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Take a breather at Vasona Lake. Walk the easy trail around the lake, let the kids run loose, and if timing works out, catch the little train they absolutely love.
cards.why: 77 degrees and sunny is exactly what this park was made for.

cards.name: Barnes & Noble
cards.address: 122 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Barnes and Noble on N Santa Cruz Ave. Let the kids pick a book or browse the toys and games section while you grab a coffee inside.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun stop for families and keeps everyone happy before heading home.

cards.name: Palmetto Superfoods - Mountain View
cards.address: 2565 California St Suite 83, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with something fresh at Palmetto Superfoods. Grab a smoothie bowl or a quick breakfast bite to get the day going.
cards.why: Good fuel before a full day out.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Dig into the Computer History Museum. The Steve Jobs in Exile exhibit is here too, so give yourself a solid two hours to wander through both.
cards.why: Even if you're not a tech person, the history on display is genuinely wild and worth the admission.

cards.name: Book Launch with Randy Ribay
cards.address: 265 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Head over to Book Passage in Los Altos for Randy Ribay's book launch at 11 AM. Free, casual, and worth popping in for.
cards.why: It's a fun low-key way to break up the day with something literary.

cards.name: Seasons Noodles & Dumplings Garden
cards.address: 702 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Lunch at Seasons Noodles and Dumplings Garden on Villa St. Order a bowl of noodles and a plate of dumplings and take your time with it.
cards.why: Solid comfort food at a great price for a Saturday afternoon.

cards.name: South Bay Ceramics - Studio 1954
cards.address: 1954 Old Middlefield Way STE I, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into South Bay Ceramics Studio 1954 and see what's available for a walk-in session. Great way to spend a couple of hands-on hours.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun afternoon activity that doesn't feel like just another errand.

cards.name: Lyra Spa Massage
cards.address: 108 S Sunnyvale Ave Ste B, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a massage at Lyra Spa in Sunnyvale. Book ahead if you can, or check availability when you arrive.
cards.why: A good way to decompress after a packed day before dinner.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Let the kids run loose around the lake, watch the birds, and soak up that Bay breeze before it gets warm.
cards.why: A wide-open outdoor space is exactly what kids need first thing on a Saturday morning.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the Computer History Museum and let the kids get hands-on with how computers went from room-sized machines to pocket-sized everything. The exhibits are genuinely wild and interactive enough to hold kid attention.
cards.why: It's right there and the exhibits are way more engaging than you'd expect for the whole family.

cards.name: My Year With AI
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Check out the My Year With AI exhibition while you're already in the building. It's a great conversation starter with kids about how AI actually works in everyday life.
cards.why: You're already there, and it pairs perfectly with the main museum floor.

cards.name: HyperSpace Los Altos
cards.address: 127 1st St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Head to HyperSpace Los Altos for some active, high-energy fun. Think laser tag, arcade games, and the kind of place where kids are basically vibrating with excitement.
cards.why: Kids will have a blast and it burns off all that post-museum energy in the best possible way.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Real mummies, ancient artifacts, and surprisingly deep Egyptian history — give yourself a couple of hours to wander the collection and don't skip the tomb replica.
cards.why: It's one of those places you keep meaning to check out and never do, and today's the day.

cards.name: Football - San Jose State vs. 2026 Spring Game at San Jose, Calif.
cards.blurb: Head over to SJSU for the Spring Game — free admission, outdoor stadium, and a chance to see the team before fall season kicks off.
cards.why: Free football on a 79-degree afternoon is a solid use of a Saturday.

cards.name: Nabi Cat Cafe
cards.address: 2255 The Alameda, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Post-game stop at Nabi Cat Cafe — grab a drink, hang out with the cats, and decompress in one of the coziest spots around.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun detour and the cats make everything better.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row as the evening crowd fills in, browse the shops, and grab dinner at one of the patio restaurants along the boulevard.
cards.why: The outdoor energy here on a warm Saturday evening is hard to beat.

cards.name: Hapa’s Brewing Company
cards.address: 59 Barack Obama Blvd, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Cap the night at Hapa's Brewing Company with a pint or two and whatever's on tap — laid-back vibe, great beer, and a solid way to end a full day.
cards.why: A local craft brewery is the right call for a Saturday night wind-down.

cards.name: Alum Rock Park
cards.address: 15350 Penitencia Creek Rd, San Jose, CA 95127, USA
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Alum Rock Park while the morning is still cool. The creek trail is easy enough for kids and the canyon views are way better than you'd expect from a city park.
cards.why: Perfect temperature for a morning hike before it warms up.

cards.name: Teen Book Sale
cards.address: 6445 Camden Ave. San Jose
cards.blurb: The Teen Book Sale at Camden is a great browse for older kids. Cheap prices, real finds, and it's the kind of place where kids actually get excited to pick something out.
cards.why: Free to get in and a great way to let the kids pick their own books.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum for real mummies, sarcophagi, and hieroglyphics. Kids usually go wide-eyed the second they walk in. The outdoor garden is a nice bonus at the end.
cards.why: It is genuinely one of the coolest things in San Jose and kids never expect it.

cards.name: Covour Coffee
cards.address: 2706 Middlefield Rd, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Covour Coffee with a solid cup and something to eat before the day gets going.
cards.why: Perfect low-key start to a Saturday morning.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hike the Dish Trail loop for big views over the Bay and the foothills. The climb is worth it and the weather today is ideal for it.
cards.why: 57 to 77 degrees is about as good as it gets for a hike like this.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through the Computer History Museum and let yourself get genuinely sucked in. The exhibits cover everything from punch cards to the internet and it never feels dry.
cards.why: Great way to spend a couple hours indoors after being outside all morning.

cards.name: Book Launch with Randy Ribay
cards.address: 265 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Head over to Linden Tree Books in Los Altos for the Randy Ribay book launch. Free, casual, and a nice way to spend an early evening with a good author in a great indie bookshop.
cards.why: Free event at one of the best indie bookstores around.

cards.name: Sundance The Steakhouse
cards.address: 1921 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: End the night with dinner at Sundance the Steakhouse. Get a proper steak, settle into the booth, and take your time with it.
cards.why: You earned a real sit-down dinner after a full day like this.

cards.name: Hatched
cards.address: 855 El Camino Real #161, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Hatched, a cozy Palo Alto breakfast spot great for families. Grab egg dishes and something for the kids before the day gets going.
cards.why: A solid breakfast sets everyone up for a full day of activities.

cards.name: Family Fun Day: Travel and Transport
cards.blurb: Head to Los Altos for Family Fun Day focused on travel and transport. Expect hands-on activities, exhibits, and interactive stuff the kids will genuinely geek out over.
cards.why: Free, kid-focused, and happening today — hard to beat that combo.
cards.venue: Los Altos Community Center (97 Hillview Ave)

cards.name: Art for All Family Day, Spring 2026
cards.address: 328 Lomita Drive, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Art for All Family Day at the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus. There are hands-on art activities designed for kids, so let them make something and wander the galleries together.
cards.why: Free family art activities on a beautiful campus Saturday is exactly the kind of thing you'll be glad you didn't skip.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the afternoon at the Computer History Museum in Mountain View. Walk through the exhibits on early computers, see the massive collection of machines, and let the kids try the interactive displays.
cards.why: Kids are genuinely surprised by how wild tech history is, and the exhibits are hands-on enough to keep them engaged.

cards.name: South Bay Ceramics - Studio 1954
cards.address: 1954 Old Middlefield Way STE I, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into South Bay Ceramics in Mountain View for an open studio session. Kids can get their hands in clay and make something to take home.
cards.why: A creative wind-down activity that kids love and parents don't have to set up or clean up at home.
